bead.title: axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client
bead.description:
Per the TD from B_TD: implement the axon backend in cli/internal/bead/backend_axon.go (or per the existing backend.go shape). Use genqlient to generate a typed Go client from Axon's GraphQL introspection schema — no protoc/protobuf toolchain. Wire as a selectable backend (config flag .ddx/config.yaml backend: axon|jsonl|bd). Default stays jsonl Day 1. Existing tests must pass against both backends.

Subscription support: implement a subscription client (genqlient supports subscriptions via separate library, or use a simple WebSocket GraphQL subscriptions client) so that DDx server can subscribe to Axon ChangeEvents on ddx_beads / ddx_bead_events for live UI updates.
bead.acceptance: 1. backend_axon.go lands. 2. genqlient-generated client at cli/internal/bead/axon/. 3. Selectable via .ddx/config.yaml. 4. Default unchanged (jsonl). 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes against both backends (parameterized test). 6. chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend. 7. WebSocket subscription client present and exercised by an integration test.

All 7 acceptance criteria are already satisfied in the current HEAD (60bee3ffb):

1. backend_axon.go — cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go (18 KB, tracked in git)

4. Default unchanged — store.go:94 defaults to BackendJSONL
5. Tests pass against both backends — backend_conformance_test.go parametrizes jsonl and axon
6. chaos_test.go — chaosBackends() runs both "jsonl" and "axon" paths
7. WebSocket subscriptions — axon/subscription.go + axon/subscription_smoke_test.go

status=open
reason=retryable All acceptance criteria for ddx-8d747049 are already implemented by its closed child beads; no new code changes are needed in this attempt. AC verification against current tree (base-rev 91b46bf0d1b53b99f8e54a6859a52d15f14725cb): 1. backend_axon.go lands -> cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go (committed by ddx-8bf23be0 / ddx-9c5bca8f) 2. genqlient-generated client at cli/internal/bead/axon/ -> cli/internal/bead/axon/{generated.go,genqlient.yaml,schema.graphql, queries/bead.graphql,queries/change_events.graphql} (ddx-8bf23be0) 3. Selectable via .ddx/config.yaml -> cli/internal/bead/store.go:98-137 honors bead_tracker.backend (env DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or config); .ddx/config.yaml documents the bead_tracker.backend: axon block (ddx-29f02cf4) 4. Default unchanged (jsonl) -> cli/internal/bead/store.go:99-103: backendType == "" -> BackendJSONL 5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes against both backends (parameterized test) -> chaos_test.go:38-48 parameterizes {jsonl, axon} across the shared backend matrix (ddx-958b8fc3). A full-package run on this host (load average ~60-68, ~175 concurrent ddx/go/claude processes from other concurrent executions sharing this machine) hit two contention-induced failures: - TestChaos_LargeScaleConcurrentCreateReadAll: "bead: lock timeout (owner pid: <other live pid>)" for both jsonl and axon subtests. Re-run in isolation immediately after: both subtests PASS (axon 5.22s, jsonl 21.19s) with no code changes. - TestPerformance_BeadStoreConcurrentClaimReadyHeartbeatUnderBudget: p99 lock wait 3095ms vs 3000ms budget (full run), then failed again on isolated re-run with "lock timeout (owner pid: <other live pid>)" -- consistent with CPU/disk scheduling contention from unrelated concurrent processes on this shared host, not a defect in the axon backend or its tests. internal/bead/axon, internal/bead/internal/storage, and internal/bead/ops/queue subpackages passed cleanly in the same run. 6. chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend -> TestChaos_LargeScaleConcurrentCreateReadAll/axon passes when the host is not under contention (see above); the failure observed in this attempt is transient host load, not a code regression. 7. WebSocket subscription client present and exercised by an integration test -> cli/internal/bead/axon/subscription.go (SubscriptionClient, ChangeEvents, SubscribeLifecycle) exercised by TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream and TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsMapToLifecycle in cli/internal/bead/axon/client_test.go (ddx-8dd19492), plus the GraphQL boundary test TestAxonBackend_GraphQLClientBoundary in cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go. No source changes were made in this attempt. Recommend retrying `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` when this host is under normal load; the two failures above are attributable to shared-machine contention (other pids holding locks / consuming CPU), not to the axon backend implementation.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

AC verification against current tree (base-rev 91b46bf0d1b53b99f8e54a6859a52d15f14725cb):

1. backend_axon.go lands
   -> cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go (committed by ddx-8bf23be0 / ddx-9c5bca8f)

2. genqlient-generated client at cli/internal/bead/axon/
   -> cli/internal/bead/axon/{generated.go,genqlient.yaml,schema.graphql,
      queries/bead.graphql,queries/change_events.graphql} (ddx-8bf23be0)

3. Selectable via .ddx/config.yaml
   -> cli/internal/bead/store.go:98-137 honors bead_tracker.backend (env
      DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or config); .ddx/config.yaml documents the
      bead_tracker.backend: axon block (ddx-29f02cf4)

4. Default unchanged (jsonl)
   -> cli/internal/bead/store.go:99-103: backendType == "" -> BackendJSONL

5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes against both backends
   (parameterized test)
   -> chaos_test.go:38-48 parameterizes {jsonl, axon} across the shared
      backend matrix (ddx-958b8fc3). A full-package run on this host
      (load average ~60-68, ~175 concurrent ddx/go/claude processes from
      other concurrent executions sharing this machine) hit two
      contention-induced failures:
        - TestChaos_LargeScaleConcurrentCreateReadAll: "bead: lock timeout
          (owner pid: <other live pid>)" for both jsonl and axon subtests.
          Re-run in isolation immediately after: both subtests PASS
          (axon 5.22s, jsonl 21.19s) with no code changes.
        - TestPerformance_BeadStoreConcurrentClaimReadyHeartbeatUnderBudget:
          p99 lock wait 3095ms vs 3000ms budget (full run), then failed
          again on isolated re-run with "lock timeout (owner pid: <other
          live pid>)" -- consistent with CPU/disk scheduling contention
          from unrelated concurrent processes on this shared host, not a
          defect in the axon backend or its tests.
      internal/bead/axon, internal/bead/internal/storage, and
      internal/bead/ops/queue subpackages passed cleanly in the same run.

6. chaos_test.go suite passes against axon backend
   -> TestChaos_LargeScaleConcurrentCreateReadAll/axon passes when the host
      is not under contention (see above); the failure observed in this
      attempt is transient host load, not a code regression.

7. WebSocket subscription client present and exercised by an integration
   test
   -> cli/internal/bead/axon/subscription.go (SubscriptionClient,
      ChangeEvents, SubscribeLifecycle) exercised by
      TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsStream and
      TestAxonSubscription_ChangeEventsMapToLifecycle in
      cli/internal/bead/axon/client_test.go (ddx-8dd19492), plus the
      GraphQL boundary test TestAxonBackend_GraphQLClientBoundary in
      cli/internal/bead/axon_backend_test.go.

No source changes were made in this attempt. Recommend retrying
`cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/...` when this host is under normal
load; the two failures above are attributable to shared-machine contention
(other pids holding locks / consuming CPU), not to the axon backend
implementation.
